new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage of heading 2208 that is a single malt Scotch whisky: produced in pot stills at a single distillery, by distilling a fermented mash consisting exclusively of malted barley.
A distillation process carried out in traditional pot stills (Brennblasen), as required for the production of single malt Scotch whisky at a single distillery.

new_classes.label_en: fermented mash of malted barley (single malt whisky feedstock)
new_classes.label_de: vergorene Maische aus gemälzter Gerste (Single Malt Whisky-Rohstoff)
new_classes.definition_en: A fermented mash consisting exclusively of malted barley, used as the sole feedstock in the distillation of single malt Scotch whisky.
A quality borne by a spirit indicating that it was produced entirely at a single distillery, as required for classification as single malt Scotch whisky.

coverage_explanation: The four main distinguishing criteria from the legal text are covered: (1) pot still distillation process (PotStillDistillationProcess class + hasDistillationProcess restriction), (2) single distillery provenance (SingleDistilleryOriginQuality class + has_quality restriction), (3) fermented mash of exclusively malted barley as feedstock (MaltedBarleyMash class + hasDistillationFeedstock restriction), and (4) parent membership in SpiritousBeverage2208. A minor gap remains: the 'exclusively malted barley' constraint (no other grain permitted) is expressed as a named class but cannot be fully formalised as a closed-world complement axiom in OWL 2 DL without a nominals-based approach, leading to a small reduction from 1.0. The Scotch whisky geographical origin (Scotland) is implied by the classification context but not explicitly axiomatised, as no GeographicalRegion class for Scotland exists in the current TBox.
